title: Email Deliverability, Sender Reputation, DKIM/SPF/DMARC Authentication, and Inbox-Placement Telemetry
dataSources:
Unified envelope in **index=business** after Heavy Forwarder parsing — each bullet pairs sourcetype with ingestion spine.
• Twilio **SendGrid** Event Webhook HTTPS POST JSON arrays (`processed`, `deferred`, `delivered`, `bounce`, `dropped`, `spam_report`, `unsubscribe`) terminating on **HTTP Event Collector** → **`sendgrid:event_webhook`** — optional Suppression APIs (`/v3/suppression/bounces`, `/v3/suppression/spam_reports`, `/v3/suppression/blocks`) mirrored hourly into companion sourcetypes **`sendgrid:bounce_report`**, **`sendgrid:suppression`** for hygiene coverage math.
• **Mailgun** `/v3/{domain}/events` plus `/v3/{domain}/bounces` OAuth pollers or webhooks → **`mailgun:event`**, **`mailgun:bounce`** with `severity` preserving hard versus soft semantics.
• **Postmark** `/bounces` + bounce webhook → **`postmark:bounce_webhook`**; server stats API optional for throughput baselines.
• **Amazon SES** Event Publishing (Configuration Sets → **SNS**, **CloudWatch**, **Kinesis**) → **`aws:ses:event_publishing`** retaining `eventType`, `bounce`, `complaint`, `mail` nested headers.
• **SparkPost** `/api/v1/messaging-events` → **`sparkpost:messaging_event`**; `/api/v1/metrics/deliverability/aggregate` snapshots optional.
• **Mailjet**, **SocketLabs**, **ElasticEmail**, **Resend** webhook lanes normalized via Add-on Builder modular inputs → vendor sourcetypes **`mailjet:message_event`**, **`socketlabs:message_sent`**, **`resend:webhook`** when procurement standardized DevOps-centric stacks.
• **DMARC** aggregate XML via dmarcian APIs, Postmark DMARC Monitoring pulls, Agari/EasyDMARC exports → **`dmarc:aggregate_report`** parsed fields **`spf_auth_results.type`**, **`dkim_auth_results.type`**, **`policy_evaluated`** nodes.
• **Google Postmaster Tools** bulk CSV/API extracts → **`google_postmaster:domain_metrics`** surfacing spam-rate, authentication pass shares, encryption ratios.
• **Validity Sender Score** REST lookups → **`validity:sender_score`** with numeric **`score`** (0–100) keyed by **`published_ip`**.
• **Litmus**, **EmailOnAcid**, **Glock Apps** inbox-placement APIs → **`litmus:inbox_placement`**, **`emailonacid:test_result`**, **`glockapps:spam_test`** exposing **`seed_provider`**, **`folder_placement`** enums.
• Optional MTA syslog (**Postfix** **`smtpd`**, Cisco **IronPort ESA**) → **`syslog:mail`** / **`ironport:mail_logs`** correlating **`queue_id`** with ESP **`smtp-id`** when hybrid relays fan marketing traffic.
app: Splunk HTTP Event Collector ([Splunk Docs — Use HTTP Event Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/UsetheHTTPEventCollector)) + Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) for warehouse DMARC mart snapshots + Splunk Add-on Builder / modular REST/TCP listeners for SES Kinesis transforms, dmarcian aggregate downloads, Litmus OAuth clients, Validity Sender Score polling, Google Postmaster bulk exports.
spl:
index=business (sourcetype="sendgrid:event_webhook" OR sourcetype="mailgun:event" OR sourcetype="aws:ses:event_publishing" OR sourcetype="sparkpost:messaging_event" OR sourcetype="dmarc:aggregate_report" OR sourcetype="google_postmaster:domain_metrics" OR sourcetype="validity:sender_score" OR sourcetype="litmus:inbox_placement") earliest=-30d@d
| eval evt=lower(trim(coalesce(event_type,eventType,notification_type,ses_event_type,messaging_event_type,type,"")))
| eval bounce_cat=lower(trim(coalesce(bounce_classification,bounce_type,bounce_subtype,bounce_reason,bounced_classification,"")))
| eval sending_domain=lower(trim(coalesce(mail_from_domain,sending_domain,domain,dmarc_policy_domain,identity_name,"")))
| eval ip_addr=trim(coalesce(sending_ip,outbound_ip,ses_source_ip,remote_ip,""))
| eval sent_evt=if(match(evt,"processed|send|queued|accepted"),1,0)
| eval delivered_evt=if(match(evt,"delivered|delivery"),1,0)
| eval bounce_evt=if(match(evt,"bounce|reject") OR match(bounce_cat,"suppress|invalid|blocked|spam"),1,0)
| eval complaint_evt=if(match(evt,"spamreport|complaint|feedback") OR match(bounce_cat,"complaint|abuse"),1,0)
| eval dropped_evt=if(match(evt,"dropped"),1,0)
| eval defer_evt=if(match(evt,"deferred|delayed"),1,0)
| eval hard_bounce=if(match(bounce_cat,"suppress|invalid|blocked|hard|unknown.?user"),1,0)
| eval soft_bounce=if(match(bounce_cat,"quota|defer|technical|timeout|greylist"),1,0)
| eval dmarc_pass_share=tonumber(coalesce(dmarc_pct_pass,policy_pct_pass,count_dmarc_pass,""))
| eval dkim_pass_flag=if(match(lower(trim(coalesce(dkim_result,parsed_dkim,""))),"pass"),1,0)
| eval spf_pass_flag=if(match(lower(trim(coalesce(spf_result,parsed_spf,""))),"pass"),1,0)
| eval inbox_pct=tonumber(coalesce(inbox_placement_pct,inbox_share_pct,seeds_inbox_pct,""))
| eval sender_score=tonumber(coalesce(validity_sender_score,ip_score,reputation_numeric,""))
| stats sum(sent_evt) as sends sum(delivered_evt) as delivered sum(bounce_evt) as bounces sum(complaint_evt) as complaints sum(dropped_evt) as dropped sum(defer_evt) as deferred sum(hard_bounce) as hard_ct sum(soft_bounce) as soft_ct sum(dkim_pass_flag) as dkim_pass_ct sum(spf_pass_flag) as spf_pass_ct avg(dmarc_pass_share) as dmarc_pass_avg avg(inbox_pct) as inbox_placement_avg avg(sender_score) as avg_sender_score dc(ip_addr) as egress_ips by sending_domain
| eval denom=max(sends,delivered+bounces+dropped)
| eval bounce_rate_pct=if(denom>0, round(100*bounces/denom,4), null())
| eval complaint_rate_pct=if(delivered>0, round(100*complaints/delivered,5), null())
| eval acceptance_pct=if((delivered+bounces+dropped)>0, round(100*delivered/(delivered+bounces+dropped),3), null())
| eval auth_health_pct=round(dmarc_pass_avg,3)
| eval deliverability_alarm=case(bounce_rate_pct>5 OR complaint_rate_pct>0.1 OR avg_sender_score<50 OR inbox_placement_avg<71,"severe", bounce_rate_pct>2 OR complaint_rate_pct>0.03 OR avg_sender_score<70 OR inbox_placement_avg<83.6,"elevated", bounce_rate_pct>1 OR complaint_rate_pct>0.01 OR avg_sender_score<80,"watch", true(),"steady")
| sort + deliverability_alarm - bounce_rate_pct
| table sending_domain sends delivered deferred dropped hard_ct soft_ct bounce_rate_pct complaint_rate_pct acceptance_pct inbox_placement_avg dmarc_pass_avg avg_sender_score auth_health_pct egress_ips deliverability_alarm
description: This Splunk pattern unions **ESP** webhook timelines (**sendgrid:event_webhook**, **mailgun:event**, **sparkpost:messaging_event**), **AWS SES** Event Publishing (**aws:ses:event_publishing**) rows with **bounceType**/**bounceSubtype** semantics, parsed **DMARC** aggregate XML (**dmarc:aggregate_report**) exposing **spf_result**, **dkim_result**, **policy_evaluated/disposition**, Google Postmaster exports (**google_postmaster:domain_metrics**) for spam-rate and authentication summaries, Validity-style **sender_score** polls (**validity:sender_score**), and Litmus-style inbox seeds (**litmus:inbox_placement**). It computes **bounce_rate_pct**, **complaint_rate_pct**, **acceptance_pct**, **dmarc_pass_avg**, **inbox_placement_avg**, **avg_sender_score**, and **deliverability_alarm** tiers referencing benchmark rails—Validity **2024 State of Email Deliverability** global inbox placement ~83.6%, Sender Score bands (<70 elevated risk), AWS guidance on bounce >5% account pause and complaint >0.1% pause—rather than creative engagement ratios tracked separately under UC-23.3.3.
value: Messaging infrastructure and brand-security leadership instrument **RFC 6376 DKIM**, **RFC 7208 SPF**, **RFC 7489 DMARC**, **RFC 8461 MTA-STS**, **RFC 8460 TLS-RPT**, **ARC**, and **BIMI** readiness alongside reputation telemetry before Gmail/Yahoo enforcement thresholds silently throttle campaigns—protecting pipeline attributed upstream when acceptance—not clicks—breaks first. Splunk aligns webhook-grade telemetry with aggregate DMARC processors (dmarcian, Postmark DMARC Monitoring, Agari-class tooling), Postmaster dashboards, Microsoft SNDS counters, Spamhaus lookups, and AWS SES reputation dashboards so incident bridges cite common identifiers (**sending_domain**, **message_id**, Mail Transfer Agent queue identifiers). When complaint spikes precede CRM-visible dips, Legal and Deliverability guilds coordinate suppression audits tied to CASL/GDPR unsubscribe artifacts without confusing this UC with cross-channel ROI (UC-23.3.1), funnel waterfalls (UC-23.3.2), engagement KPIs (UC-23.3.3), SEO splits (UC-23.3.4), paid CPA tiles (UC-23.3.5), or resource-center conversion lifts (UC-23.3.6).
implementation: Terminate ESP HTTPS POST payloads on Heavy Forwarder **HTTP Event Collector** tokens scoped to index=business; stream SES JSON via **Kinesis Firehose→HEC** or **SNS→Lambda→HEC**; ingest DMARC aggregate XML through dmarcian/easyDMARC APIs or rsync drops parsed to **dmarc:aggregate_report**; schedule modular pulls for **validity:sender_score** and **google_postmaster** bulk exports; normalize **sending_domain** and **message_id** across vendors; publish saved search **rpt_deliverability_guardrail** plus Dashboard Studio workspace binding threshold tokens documented with Messaging Ops.
visualization: Dashboard Studio: **splunk.table** ranking **sending_domain** rows by **deliverability_alarm** then **bounce_rate_pct**; trellised **splunk.column** comparing **bounce_rate_pct**, **complaint_rate_pct**, **acceptance_pct** against benchmark overlays; **splunk.singlevalue** tiles for global **avg_sender_score** (Validity bands), **inbox_placement_avg** versus ~83.6% benchmark reference; **splunk.line** time chart for **dmarc_pass_avg** across reporting intervals; optional geographic facets when DMARC XML exposes country stamps; Cisco **IronPort** mail_logs correlates added only when correlating hybrid MTA rejects with ESP identifiers.
knownFalsePositives: Aggregate **DMARC** XML arriving on staggered forty-eight-to-seventy-two hour publisher cadences makes week-to-week pass rates dip when reporting domains batch weekend files although Mail Transfer Agent transports stayed steady. **Inbox-placement** seed tests referencing obsolete Litmus cohorts diverge fifteen to twenty-five points from consumer reality because recycled **spam-trap** pools or purchased lists were purged upstream before Splunk summarized **`litmus:inbox_placement`**. **AWS SES** configuration-set publishing duplicates identical **`message_id`** rows when SNS subscribers redrive after SQS visibility timeouts, doubling **`bounce_evt`** counts until **`dedup`** macros keyed on **`message_id`** plus **`notification_type`** suppress repeats. **Dedicated IP** migrations advertising fresh **`PTR`** hosts lag DNS caches for several days—Talos lookups temporarily flag poor reputation despite mailbox providers seeing aligned **SPF** includes. **Google Postmaster Tools** spam-rate telemetry trails webhook complaints two reporting intervals during peak retail bursts—Splunk alarms first while Postmaster dashboards remain green until nightly snapshots ingest. **BIMI** logo retrieval failures tied to oversized **SVG Tiny PSV** variants rarely alter **`DMARC`** disposition in aggregate seeds though Gmail clips logos—teams mistakenly chase **DKIM** rotations unrelated to avatar rendering. **`MTA-STS`** **`mode=testing`** paired with noisy **`TLS-RPT`** ingestion raises benign Opportunistic TLS downgrade rows across APAC carriers that never affect marketing streams. **Suppression-list** hygiene jobs that scrub **`role`** mailboxes suppress billing aliases still expecting invoices—false alarms fade only when transactional **`configuration_set_name`** dimensions split bulk from finance mail. **Spamhaus CSS** composite listings hit neighboring subnets in abusive `/24` ranges—Splunk spikes imply collateral guilt despite **`bounce_complaint`** ratios staying compliant until Abuse desks relocate egress pools.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud roles covering **index=business**, HTTP Event Collector administration, outbound HTTPS from Heavy Forwarders to ESP webhook origins (Twilio SendGrid mutual-TLS endpoints where your account team mandates them), AWS IAM credentials for SES APIs plus optional **Kinesis Data Firehose** or **Amazon SNS** subscribers landing JSON near-real-time beside batch reputation exports.
• Parser discipline for RFC 7489 aggregate XML (`<feedback><record>...</record>`) versus JSON-wrapped forensic feeds—never merge forensic personally identifiable payloads into executive dashboards without Legal approval; keep forensic **ruf** mailboxes segregated per privacy counsel.
• Separate credential scopes for Google Postmaster Tools downloads versus Gmail Admin APIs; bind exports per **Authorized Domain** so Splunk rows preserve **domain reputation** enums (**HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW|BAD**) that match Postmaster UI tiles.
• Benchmark pack pinned in runbook: Validity **2024 State of Email Deliverability** (global inbox placement mean 83.6%, top-quartile near 95%, bottom-quartile near 71%); Sender Score reputation benchmark (~84 ESP IP average with >90 as top-decile band); dmarcian **2024 DMARC Adoption Report** policy adoption facts; AWS SES bounce>5% and complaint>0.1% automated pause thresholds; Google Postmaster spam-rate rails (<0.10% recommended; >0.30% degraded; >1.0% danger); **NIST SP 800-177** trustworthy email guidance paired with **CISA BOD 18-01** when federal programs appear in stakeholder reviews.
• DNS change tickets must log **DKIM selector** publishes, SPF flattening edits, **DMARC pct=** ramps, BIMI **VMC** anchor updates, `_mta-sts.` TXT rotations, and `_smtp._tls` **TLS-RPT** endpoints because Splunk spikes frequently correlate with cutovers—not faulty parsers.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
**SendGrid/Twilio:** Register Event Webhook POST targets hitting Splunk HTTP Event Collector (`/services/collector/event`) with JSON arrays carrying **event** ∈ processed|deferred|delivered|bounce|dropped|spam_report|... — preserve `smtp-id`, **reason**, **status**, `bounce_classification` verbatim in `props.conf` FIELDALIAS staging before SPL maps synonyms.
**Mailgun:** Poll `/v3/{domain}/events` or terminate webhook payloads → `mailgun:event` with **severity=temporary|permanent** bounce distinctions mirrored into **bounce_cat**.
**Amazon SES:** Enable Event Publishing on Configuration Sets with destinations **SNS** topics or **CloudWatch** metric streams; normalize **eventType** Send|Reject|Bounce|Complaint|Delivery|DeliveryDelay into `aws:ses:event_publishing` sourcetype with **bounceType/bounceSubtype** fields retained per AWS SES Event Publishing documentation.
**SparkPost:** Pull `/api/v1/messaging-events` or Signals-compatible feeds → `sparkpost:messaging_event`; retain **transactional** versus **recipient-domain** facets for ESP-vs-recipient splits referenced when diagnosing mailbox-provider bucketing.
**Postmark:** Capture `/bounces` plus bounce webhook payloads → **postmark:bounce_webhook** rows carrying **ID** integers distinct from Mailgun schemas—keep numeric identifiers separate when deduping replays.
**Mailchimp Transactional:** Mandrill-style webhook bursts (`mandrill:event`) retain hard versus soft bounce verbs essential for taxonomy macros separate from Marketing reports reserved for UC-23.3.3 engagement KPIs.
**DMARC processors:** Configure dmarcian aggregate ingestion or inbound mailbox parsers dumping XML to `dmarc:aggregate_report` including **spf_auth_results**, **dkim_auth_results**, **policy_evaluated**. Surface ARC **i=** intermediaries when mailing lists break alignment so Splunk distinguishes forwarding versus spoofing.
**Reputation scores:** Poll Validity Sender Score REST lookups (`senderscore.com` documented endpoints) → `validity:sender_score`; optionally enrich Spamhaus CSS hits or **Talos** qualitative lookups via lookups keyed on **ip_addr**.
**Inbox placement:** Land Litmus `/v2/results` exports or EmailOnAcid `/api/v5/email-tests` payloads → `litmus:inbox_placement` / **emailonacid:test_result** with **seed_provider** tags; layer Glock Apps spam tests (**glockapps:spam_test**) when procurement standardized multiple vendors.
**MTA corroboration:** Optional **syslog** tail of Postfix **smtpd**/**qmgr** lines with **queue_id** joins or Cisco IronPort ESA mail_logs when hybrid architectures fan-in marketing relays—distinct from MAP nurture instrumentation.

Additional ingestion hygiene — Customer.io transactional lanes (`/v1/send/email`) often emit **`customerio:delivery_event`** streams—exclude those sourcetypes when judging bulk pools unless dashboards intentionally blend journeys; tag **`configuration_set_name`** from SES so transactional receipts remain comparable separately from blast sends required by Gmail/Yahoo bulk guidelines.

**Step 2 — Encode macros, lookups, and the primary saved search**
Author `macros.conf` helpers: **`deliver_normalize_event()`** mapping vendor synonyms (`spam_complaint`, `spamreport`, `complaint`) onto `complaint_evt`; **`bounce_taxonomy()`** collapsing RFC 3463 codes into hard versus soft buckets aligned with SES dictionaries including **mailbox full** versus **bad mailbox** distinctions emphasized in bounce remediation guides.
Maintain CSV lookup **`esp_shared_pool.csv`** tagging whether **ip_addr** sits on dedicated versus pooled egress—Splunk overlays pool semantics before interpreting Sender Score deltas during warmup ramps guided by Postmaster scaling narratives (incremental volume ladders across multiple weeks).
Persist KV Store checkpoints for modular REST polls (`last_dmarc_report_id`, `postmaster_snapshot_ts`, **`last_kinesis_sequence`**) so retries never duplicate aggregate counts that falsely crater `dmarc_pass_avg` after SNS replays.
Clone SPL from the canonical **spl** field into saved search **`rpt_deliverability_guardrail`**; chain companion **`alert_deliverability_severe`** filtering **`deliverability_alarm="severe"`** with throttle twelve hours per `sending_domain` unless bounce spikes exceed SES pause thresholds (route immediately to Messaging Ops).

**Step 3 — Validate against authoritative consoles**
Cross-check Splunk `bounce_rate_pct` against Twilio SendGrid Activity diagnostics for identical **subusers** within ±two percent tolerance bounded by webhook backlog lag documented on Twilio status hooks.
Replay SES samples via AWS SES reputation dashboard bounce and complaint percentages—ensure Splunk denominators exclude SNS duplicates using **`stats dc(message_id)`** spot checks aligned with AWS guidance on pause thresholds.
Compare `dmarc_pass_avg` against dmarcian or Postmark DMARC Monitoring dashboards per organizational domain—variance beyond five points usually traces to `_time` normalization when Splunk stamps ingestion while XML `<date_range>` spans Friday-through-Sunday batches.
Verify `avg_sender_score` pullbacks against Validity web snapshots for the same **published_ip**—remember Sender Score emphasizes IPv4 senders while some ESP tunnels IPv6; annotate dashboards when IPv6 lacks coverage so executives do not infer missing intelligence as zero risk.
Litmus inbox placement outcomes: align **inbox_placement_avg** with vendor UI seed lists—if Litmus cohorts omit Yahoo seeds refreshed after DNS edits, mismatches arise unrelated to SMTP acceptance curves recorded elsewhere.

**Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and routing**
Publish Dashboard Studio workspace **`email_infra_guardrails`** binding visualizations from the **visualization** field—default trailing thirty days, trellis by **ESP** vendor token plus **`configuration_set_name`** when SES segregates lanes for regulatory versus promotional streams.
Route `severe` alarms to Messaging Infra plus Brand Security paging bridges; **elevated** notifications feed weekly deliverability guild tickets referencing PTR, **DKIM selector**, **DMARC policy** hyperlinks mirrored from DNS change tools (**dmarcian**, **Valimail**, **OnDMARC** deployments).
Embed **suppression-list** hygiene tiles sourced from **`sendgrid:suppression`** / **`mailgun:suppression`** extracts showing bounce/spam-report/invalidate buckets—distinct from newsletter engagement canvases owned by UC-23.3.3 because creative KPIs hide acceptance erosion until acceptance telemetry proves inbox failure first.
Footnote panels cite Cloudflare Email Security former Area 1 phishing-volume statistics only when phishing-defense stakeholders join bridges—keep statistical narratives separate from Splunk ratios unless SOC merges datasets deliberately.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting misleading Splunk indicators**
• Warmup miscount: IP addresses freshly allocated through SES or SendGrid pools still show `severe` bounce spikes while **Sender Score** sits `null`—await Validity indexing or seed HetrixTools black-list monitors before treating noise as lasting reputational collapse tied to creative teams.
• DMARC aggregate lag: Reporting domains ship weekly bundles Sunday night; Splunk `_time` stamped at arrival suggests sudden authentication cliffs although DNS stayed stable—normalize using `<date_range><begin>` epoch macros supplied by dmarcian parsers.
• SES SNS duplication: Visibility-timeout retries replay identical JSON blobs doubling `bounce_evt` counts—collapse via **`dedup message_id`** windows spanning ninety seconds minimum when SNS subscribers fan out twice during congestion bursts.
• SPF alignment confusion: Third-party envelope domains (`mail.partner.net`) diverge from strict DMARC header-from alignment expectations—evaluate **`aspf`** relaxed modes versus **`adkim`** strict couples before Splunk asserts spoofing absent corroborating forensic rows.
• DKIM dual selectors: Parallel **`s=` selectors** during rotations inflate transient authentication mismatches inside DMARC XML even though receivers ultimately validate—suppress paging while documented rotation tickets remain **Open** within forty-eight-hour SLA windows.
• BIMI VMC gaps: Missing verified mark certificates keep brand avatars absent even when DMARC reports pass—do not escalate transport teams to fix SPF when the issue remains `svg`/`a=` URL compliance.
• TLS-RPT clutter: Opportunistic TLS downgrades across regional carriers flood RFC 8460 JSON feeds with STARTTLS failures unrelated to corporate MTA policies—filter feeds using **`organization_name`** equals **your_org** before dashboards imply SMTP interception internally.
• Spamhaus CSS listings: Composite snowshoe listings attach to abusive IPs neighboring yours inside `/24` allocations—cross-check Spamhaus portal commentary before insisting DNS ownership flaws caused Splunk spikes alone.
• Microsoft SNDS divergence: Outlook telemetry volumes disagree with Gmail complaint webhook totals because Hotmail **JMR** loops lag behind Gmail Postmaster spam-rate snapshots—triangulate SNDS IP charts alongside Splunk ratios prior to blaming ESP queues exclusively.
• Litmus geography drift: EU-heavy seeds skew spam-folder percentages tighter than United States cohorts expecting Gmail promotions tabs—annotate geographic cohort filters inside inbox-placement charts before executives judge vendor swaps prematurely.
• Role-account suppression: Automated suppression engines scrub CFO-forward aliases flagged as role addresses despite transactional receipts requiring uninterrupted SMTP acceptance—exclude transactional **`configuration_set_name`** slices referenced earlier before remediation escalates unnecessarily.
• Shared pool bleed: Peer tenants blasting phishing bursts across pooled egress IPs suppress `avg_sender_score` even though **your** bounce complaint ratios remain compliant—cross-verify ESP abuse announcements prior to accusing internal CRM imports wholesale.
grandmaExplanation: We watch whether mailbox providers accept our mail, trust our signatures, and whether scores from reputation feeds slip before anyone notices quieter inboxes—not merely whether folks tapped a headline in a newsletter.
